summarization.a: The line chart illustrates the linear decrease in thickness of WO₃ with increasing number of cycles at 207°C. The text annotations indicate that W acts as an etch stop.

a.question: What is the etch rate of WO3 and how can this be determined?
a.answer: a	The etch rate can be determined by calculating the slope, which gives a value around 5 angstrom per cycle.

a.question: Why does W act as an etch stop?
a.answer: a	The process without O2/O3 exposure is selective for WO3 w.r.t. W, so there is no etching of W.

a.question: What is the original WO3 thickness in nm?
Will there be any WO3 on the surface after the finished process and the sample has been taken out of the reactor?
a.answer: a	Yes there will be, tungsten will oxidize after exposure to ambient so there will be a thin layer of WO3 at the surface.
